steps.title: Factor out the common divisor from both terms
steps.content:
Examining the inequality $3x - 9y > 12$, both terms on the left side are divisible by 3. Factor out this common factor:
$$3x - 9y = 3(x - 3y)$$
Substitute back into the inequality:
$$3(x - 3y) > 12$$

steps.title: Divide both sides by 3 to isolate the expression
steps.content:
Divide every term by 3. Since 3 is positive, the inequality sign remains unchanged:
$$\frac{3(x - 3y)}{3} > \frac{12}{3}$$
$$x - 3y > 4$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
